message:
feat(harness,ci): add backlog quality gate and no-mock critical path gate runners (bd-1dp9.9.6, bd-mblr.3.4)

Introduce a backlog acceptance-completeness quality gate that scans the
beads issue tracker and enforces that every active critical-path issue
has explicit acceptance criteria covering three requirement categories:
unit/property tests, deterministic E2E scenarios with replay
instructions, and structured logging/metrics with trace identifiers.

The gate operates in regression mode by comparing current scan results
against a checked-in baseline (`conformance/backlog_quality_gate_baseline.json`).
Known gaps recorded in the baseline are tolerated, but any newly
introduced critical-path completeness failures cause the gate to fail.
This ratchet mechanism ensures acceptance quality never silently degrades.

New files and their purposes:

- `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/backlog_quality_gate.rs`: Core library
  module implementing issue scanning, text-based requirement detection
  (word-boundary-aware matching for unit/property tests, deterministic
  E2E, and structured logging keywords), baseline diffing, regression
  detection, and deterministic JSON/Markdown report generation. Includes
  comprehensive inline unit tests for requirement detection, baseline
  allowlisting, and deterministic output with fixed timestamps.

- `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/bin/backlog_quality_gate_runner.rs`: CLI
  binary that drives the quality gate with configurable paths for beads
  input, baseline, JSON/Markdown output, critical priority threshold,
  and optional deterministic timestamps. Exits non-zero when regression
  failures are detected.

- `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/bin/no_mock_critical_path_gate_runner.rs`:
  CLI binary for the no-mock critical path gate (bd-mblr.3.4) that
  evaluates whether critical invariants have real (non-mock) evidence.
  Supports JSON and Markdown output, optional --fail-on-warnings mode,
  and structured logging of verdict summary.

- `crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/backlog_quality_gate.rs`: Integration
  tests exercising the full gate lifecycle: schema version roundtrip
  stability, baseline regression detection (known failures tolerated,
  new failures blocked), and byte-level determinism with fixed
  timestamps.

- `conformance/backlog_quality_gate_baseline.json`: Initial baseline
  recording two known acceptance gaps (bd-3plop.6 missing structured
  logging; bd-mblr.7.1 missing unit/property tests and structured
  logging) so the gate passes in CI today while preventing new
  regressions.

- `scripts/verify_backlog_quality_gate.sh`: End-to-end shell verifier
  that creates synthetic fixtures, runs the gate binary twice to
  confirm deterministic output, injects a regression fixture to confirm
  failure detection, and optionally validates the real repository
  backlog. Supports --repo-gate and --json output modes.

Modified files:

- `crates/fsqlite-harness/src/lib.rs`: Added `pub mod backlog_quality_gate`
  to expose the new module from the harness crate.

- `.github/workflows/unit-test-shard-matrix.yml`: Added a new
  `backlog-quality-gate` job that runs the quality gate runner against
  the repository beads with the conformance baseline, plus a second
  step for the E2E verification script. Added backlog-quality-gate
  to the final check-all-shards aggregation gate. Also added a
  `no-mock-critical-path-gate` job running the no-mock gate runner
  and included it in the aggregation gate.

- `.github/workflows/verification-gates.yml`: Added two new jobs
  (`backlog-quality-gate` and `no-mock-critical-path-gate`) mirroring
  the shard matrix additions, ensuring these gates also run in the
  verification workflow with proper Rust toolchain setup and caching.

- `.beads/issues.jsonl`: Updated timestamps and status fields for
  issues bd-1dp9.9.3 (ratchet guardrails), bd-1dp9.9.6 (backlog
  quality gate - now in_progress), and bd-mblr.3.4 (no-mock critical
  path gate - now in_progress) to reflect ongoing implementation work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1dqg","title":"Add pager transaction lifecycle to Connection (begin/commit/rollback)","description":"## Goal

Wire transaction lifecycle in `fsqlite-core::Connection` to the pager/MVCC stack (BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK).

## Scope

- Begin transaction (deferred/immediate/concurrent as supported)
- Commit persists changes through the storage stack
- Rollback discards uncommitted changes
- Correct error behavior for commit/rollback without an active transaction","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance Criteria

- A simple `BEGIN; INSERT; COMMIT;` persists and is visible to a new connection.
- A simple `BEGIN; INSERT; ROLLBACK;` leaves no changes after rollback and after reopening.
- Tests cover commit/rollback without an active transaction and nested-begin rejection.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:38:04.766406712Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:10:35.275303641Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:10:35.275284225Z","close_reason":"Already implemented","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dqg","depends_on_id":"bd-3iw8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:22:58Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:20:10Z"},{"id":1673,"issue_id":"bd-1dus","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Task: Benchmark — single-threaded sequential write throughput

### What
Measure baseline write throughput: how many rows/sec can each backend INSERT sequentially in a single thread.

### Benchmark Design
- Table: `CREATE TABLE bench (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, data TEXT, value REAL)`
- Workload: 10,000 INSERT statements with prepared statements (parameterized)
- Measure: total time, derive rows/sec
- Both backends: identical schema, identical data, identical PRAGMA settings

### PRAGMA Normalization
Both databases must use identical settings:
```sql
PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;
PRAGMA synchronous = NORMAL;
PRAGMA cache_size = -64000;  -- 64MB cache
PRAGMA page_size = 4096;
```

### Variants
1. **Autocommit**: Each INSERT is its own transaction
2. **Batched**: BEGIN, 1000 INSERTs, COMMIT (10 batches)
3. **Single transaction**: BEGIN, 10000 INSERTs, COMMIT

### Expected Results
- Single-threaded: FrankenSQLite and C SQLite should be comparable (no concurrency advantage)
- Batched transactions should be faster than autocommit (fewer fsync operations)
- This establishes the baseline for concurrent benchmark comparison

### Acceptance Criteria
- Criterion benchmark with 3 variants (autocommit, batched, single-txn)
- Results show rows/sec for each variant and backend
- Baseline performance is documented
- No correctness issues (final row count verified)
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:20:43Z"},{"id":1674,"issue_id":"bd-1dus","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented write_throughput_bench.rs: 3 Criterion variants (autocommit, batched 1K/batch, single-txn) for 10K INSERT throughput. +use std::fs;
+use std::path::Path;
+
+use serde_json::Value;
+use tempfile::tempdir;
+
+use fsqlite_harness::backlog_quality_gate::{
+    BACKLOG_QUALITY_GATE_SCHEMA_VERSION, BacklogQualityBaseline, BacklogQualityBaselineEntry,
+    BacklogQualityGateConfig, RequirementKind, run_backlog_quality_gate, write_report_json,
+};
+
+const BEAD_ID: &str = "bd-1dp9.9.6";
+
+fn issue(
+    id: &str,
+    status: &str,
+    priority: i64,
+    acceptance_criteria: &str,
+    labels: &[&str],
+) -> Value {
+    serde_json::json!({
+        "id": id,
+        "title": format!("Issue {id}"),
+        "status": status,
+        "issue_type": "task",
+        "priority": priority,
+        "acceptance_criteria": acceptance_criteria,
+        "labels": labels,
+    })
+}
+
+fn write_jsonl(path: &Path, entries: &[Value]) {
+    let body = entries
+        .iter()
+        .map(|entry| serde_json::to_string(entry).expect("serialize entry"))
+        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
+        .join("\n");
+    fs::write(path, format!("{body}\n")).expect("write jsonl");
+}
+
+fn canonical_acceptance() -> &'static str {
+    "Comprehensive unit/property tests are required. Deterministic e2e scenarios \
+     with fixed seeds and replay instructions are mandatory. Structured logging/metrics \
+     must include trace_id, run_id, scenario_id, and actionable failure context."
+}
+
+#[test]
+fn report_json_schema_and_roundtrip_are_stable() {
+    let temp = tempdir().expect("tempdir");
+    let beads = temp.path().join("issues.jsonl");
+    let report_path = temp.path().join("report.json");
+    write_jsonl(
+        &beads,
+        &[issue(
+            "bd-pass",
+            "open",
+            1,
+            canonical_acceptance(),
+            &["ev-gated"],
+        )],
+    );
+
+    let report = run_backlog_quality_gate(&BacklogQualityGateConfig {
+        beads_path: beads,
+        baseline_path: None,
+        critical_priority_max: 1,
+        generated_unix_ms: Some(1_700_000_000_000),
+    })
+    .expect("gate should run");
+    write_report_json(&report_path, &report).expect("write report");
+
+    let restored: serde_json::Value =
+        serde_json::from_str(&fs::read_to_string(report_path).expect("read report"))
+            .expect("parse report");
+    assert_eq!(
+        restored["schema_version"].as_str(),
+        Some(BACKLOG_QUALITY_GATE_SCHEMA_VERSION),
+        "bead_id={BEAD_ID} case=schema_version_mismatch"
+    );
+    assert_eq!(
+        restored["overall_pass"].as_bool(),
+        Some(true),
+        "bead_id={BEAD_ID} case=overall_pass_expected_true"
+    );
+}
+
+#[test]
+fn baseline_mode_flags_only_new_critical_regressions() {
+    let temp = tempdir().expect("tempdir");
+    let beads = temp.path().join("issues.jsonl");
+    let baseline = temp.path().join("baseline.json");
+
+    let known_failure_acceptance =
+        "Unit tests only. Missing deterministic e2e seed/replay and trace_id logging.";
+    write_jsonl(
+        &beads,
+        &[
+            issue(
+                "bd-known",
+                "open",
+                1,
+                known_failure_acceptance,
+                &["ev-gated"],
+            ),
+            issue("bd-good", "open", 1, canonical_acceptance(), &["ev-gated"]),
+        ],
+    );
+    let baseline_doc = BacklogQualityBaseline {
+        schema_version: BACKLOG_QUALITY_GATE_SCHEMA_VERSION.to_owned(),
+        entries: vec![BacklogQualityBaselineEntry {
+            issue_id: "bd-known".to_owned(),
+            missing_requirements: vec![RequirementKind::DeterministicE2e],
+        }],
+    };
+    fs::write(
+        &baseline,
+        serde_json::to_string_pretty(&baseline_doc).expect("serialize baseline"),
+    )
+    .expect("write baseline");
+
+    let report_no_regression = run_backlog_quality_gate(&BacklogQualityGateConfig {
+        beads_path: beads.clone(),
+        baseline_path: Some(baseline.clone()),
+        critical_priority_max: 1,
+        generated_unix_ms: Some(1_700_000_000_123),
+    })
+    .expect("gate should run");
+    assert!(
+        report_no_regression.overall_pass,
+        "bead_id={BEAD_ID} case=known_failure_should_not_regress"
+    );
+    assert_eq!(
+        report_no_regression.regression_failures.len(),
+        0,
+        "bead_id={BEAD_ID} case=known_failure_regression_count"
+    );
+
+    write_jsonl(
+        &beads,
+        &[
+            issue(
+                "bd-known",
+                "open",
+                1,
+                known_failure_acceptance,
+                &["ev-gated"],
+            ),
+            issue("bd-new", "open", 1, "Unit tests only.", &["ev-gated"]),
+        ],
+    );
+    let report_regression = run_backlog_quality_gate(&BacklogQualityGateConfig {
+        beads_path: beads,
+        baseline_path: Some(baseline),
+        critical_priority_max: 1,
+        generated_unix_ms: Some(1_700_000_000_123),
+    })
+    .expect("gate should run");
+    assert!(
+        !report_regression.overall_pass,
+        "bead_id={BEAD_ID} case=new_failure_must_fail"
+    );
+    assert_eq!(
+        report_regression.regression_failures.len(),
+        1,
+        "bead_id={BEAD_ID} case=new_failure_regression_count"
+    );
+    assert_eq!(
+        report_regression.regression_failures[0].issue_id, "bd-new",
+        "bead_id={BEAD_ID} case=new_failure_issue_id"
+    );
+}
+
+#[test]
+fn fixed_timestamp_produces_deterministic_report_bytes() {
+    let temp = tempdir().expect("tempdir");
+    let beads = temp.path().join("issues.jsonl");
+    write_jsonl(
+        &beads,
+        &[
+            issue("bd-z", "open", 1, canonical_acceptance(), &["ev-gated"]),
+            issue("bd-a", "open", 1, "Unit tests only.", &["ev-gated"]),
+        ],
+    );
+
+    let report_a = run_backlog_quality_gate(&BacklogQualityGateConfig {
+        beads_path: beads.clone(),
+        baseline_path: None,
+        critical_priority_max: 1,
+        generated_unix_ms: Some(424_242_424_242),
+    })
+    .expect("gate should run");
+    let report_b = run_backlog_quality_gate(&BacklogQualityGateConfig {
+        beads_path: beads,
+        baseline_path: None,
+        critical_priority_max: 1,
+        generated_unix_ms: Some(424_242_424_242),
+    })
+    .expect("gate should run");
+
+    let bytes_a = serde_json::to_vec(&report_a).expect("serialize report");
+    let bytes_b = serde_json::to_vec(&report_b).expect("serialize report");
+    assert_eq!(
+        bytes_a, bytes_b,
+        "bead_id={BEAD_ID} case=deterministic_bytes_with_fixed_timestamp"
+    );
+}

+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# verify_backlog_quality_gate.sh — deterministic E2E verifier for bd-1dp9.9.6
+#
+# What this checks:
+# 1. Synthetic backlog with known baseline issue produces deterministic pass output.
+# 2. Introducing a new critical-path acceptance gap causes regression failure.
+# 3. Optional repository-mode run validates current .beads backlog against baseline.
+
+set -euo pipefail
+
+WORKSPACE_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+RUN_ROOT="$WORKSPACE_ROOT/artifacts/backlog-quality-gate-e2e"
+REPO_GATE=false
+JSON_OUTPUT=false
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+  case "$1" in
+    --repo-gate)
+      REPO_GATE=true
+      shift
+      ;;
+    --json)
+      JSON_OUTPUT=true
+      shift
+      ;;
+    *)
+      echo "ERROR: unknown argument '$1'" >&2
+      exit 2
+      ;;
+  esac
+done
+
+mkdir -p "$RUN_ROOT"
+
+if command -v rch >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+  RUNNER=(rch exec -- cargo run -p fsqlite-harness --bin backlog_quality_gate_runner --)
+else
+  RUNNER=(cargo run -p fsqlite-harness --bin backlog_quality_gate_runner --)
+fi
+
+FIXTURE_DIR="$RUN_ROOT/fixture"
+mkdir -p "$FIXTURE_DIR"
+
+FIXTURE_BEADS_PASS="$FIXTURE_DIR/issues_pass.jsonl"
+FIXTURE_BEADS_FAIL="$FIXTURE_DIR/issues_fail.jsonl"
+FIXTURE_BASELINE="$FIXTURE_DIR/baseline.json"
+REPORT_A="$FIXTURE_DIR/report_a.json"
+REPORT_B="$FIXTURE_DIR/report_b.json"
+SUMMARY_A="$FIXTURE_DIR/report_a.md"
+SUMMARY_B="$FIXTURE_DIR/report_b.md"
+REPORT_FAIL="$FIXTURE_DIR/report_fail.json"
+SUMMARY_FAIL="$FIXTURE_DIR/report_fail.md"
+FIXED_TS="1700000000000"
+
+cat > "$FIXTURE_BEADS_PASS" <<'EOF'
+{"id":"bd-fixture-known","title":"Known debt","status":"open","issue_type":"task","priority":1,"acceptance_criteria":"Unit tests only. Missing deterministic e2e seed/replay and trace_id logging.","labels":["ev-gated"]}
+{"id":"bd-fixture-good","title":"Compliant","status":"open","issue_type":"task","priority":1,"acceptance_criteria":"Comprehensive unit/property tests are required. Deterministic e2e scenarios with fixed seeds and replay instructions are mandatory. Structured logging/metrics must include trace_id, run_id, scenario_id and actionable failure context.","labels":["ev-gated"]}
+EOF
+
+cat > "$FIXTURE_BEADS_FAIL" <<'EOF'
+{"id":"bd-fixture-known","title":"Known debt","status":"open","issue_type":"task","priority":1,"acceptance_criteria":"Unit tests only. Missing deterministic e2e seed/replay and trace_id logging.","labels":["ev-gated"]}
+{"id":"bd-fixture-new","title":"New regression","status":"open","issue_type":"task","priority":1,"acceptance_criteria":"Unit tests only.","labels":["ev-gated"]}
+EOF
+
+cat > "$FIXTURE_BASELINE" <<'EOF'
+{
+  "schema_version": "1.0.0",
+  "entries": [
+    {
+      "issue_id": "bd-fixture-known",
+      "missing_requirements": ["deterministic_e2e"]
+    }
+  ]
+}
+EOF
+
+"${RUNNER[@]}" \
+  --beads "$FIXTURE_BEADS_PASS" \
+  --baseline "$FIXTURE_BASELINE" \
+  --output-json "$REPORT_A" \
+  --output-human "$SUMMARY_A" \
+  --critical-priority-max 1 \
+  --generated-unix-ms "$FIXED_TS"
+
+"${RUNNER[@]}" \
+  --beads "$FIXTURE_BEADS_PASS" \
+  --baseline "$FIXTURE_BASELINE" \
+  --output-json "$REPORT_B" \
+  --output-human "$SUMMARY_B" \
+  --critical-priority-max 1 \
+  --generated-unix-ms "$FIXED_TS"
+
+if ! diff -u "$REPORT_A" "$REPORT_B" >/dev/null; then
+  echo "ERROR: deterministic replay failed for pass fixture reports" >&2
+  diff -u "$REPORT_A" "$REPORT_B" >&2 || true
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+set +e
+"${RUNNER[@]}" \
+  --beads "$FIXTURE_BEADS_FAIL" \
+  --baseline "$FIXTURE_BASELINE" \
+  --output-json "$REPORT_FAIL" \
+  --output-human "$SUMMARY_FAIL" \
+  --critical-priority-max 1 \
+  --generated-unix-ms "$FIXED_TS"
+FAIL_EXIT=$?
+set -e
+
+if [[ $FAIL_EXIT -eq 0 ]]; then
+  echo "ERROR: expected regression fixture to fail gate" >&2
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+jq -e '.overall_pass == false' "$REPORT_FAIL" >/dev/null
+jq -e '.summary.regression_failures == 1' "$REPORT_FAIL" >/dev/null
+jq -e '.regression_failures[] | select(.issue_id == "bd-fixture-new")' "$REPORT_FAIL" >/dev/null
+
+REPO_STATUS=0
+REPO_REPORT="$RUN_ROOT/repo/report.json"
+REPO_SUMMARY="$RUN_ROOT/repo/report.md"
+
+if $REPO_GATE; then
+  mkdir -p "$RUN_ROOT/repo"
+  set +e
+  "${RUNNER[@]}" \
+    --beads "$WORKSPACE_ROOT/.beads/issues.jsonl" \
+    --baseline "$WORKSPACE_ROOT/conformance/backlog_quality_gate_baseline.json" \
+    --output-json "$REPO_REPORT" \
+    --output-human "$REPO_SUMMARY" \
+    --critical-priority-max 1
+  REPO_STATUS=$?
+  set -e
+fi
+
+if $JSON_OUTPUT; then
+  cat <<ENDJSON
+{
+  "bead_id": "bd-1dp9.9.6",
+  "deterministic_fixture_pass": true,
+  "fixture_regression_detected": true,
+  "fixture_pass_report": "${REPORT_A#$WORKSPACE_ROOT/}",
+  "fixture_regression_report": "${REPORT_FAIL#$WORKSPACE_ROOT/}",
+  "repo_gate_enabled": $REPO_GATE,
+  "repo_gate_exit_code": $REPO_STATUS,
+  "repo_report": "${REPO_REPORT#$WORKSPACE_ROOT/}"
+}
+ENDJSON
+else
+  echo "=== Backlog Quality Gate E2E ==="
+  echo "Bead ID:                     bd-1dp9.9.6"
+  echo "Deterministic pass fixture:  PASS"
+  echo "Regression fixture:          PASS (failure correctly detected)"
+  echo "Fixture pass report:         ${REPORT_A#$WORKSPACE_ROOT/}"
+  echo "Fixture fail report:         ${REPORT_FAIL#$WORKSPACE_ROOT/}"
+  if $REPO_GATE; then
+    echo "Repo gate enabled:           yes"
+    echo "Repo gate exit code:         $REPO_STATUS"
+    echo "Repo report:                 ${REPO_REPORT#$WORKSPACE_ROOT/}"
+  else
+    echo "Repo gate enabled:           no"
+  fi
+fi
+
+if $REPO_GATE && [[ $REPO_STATUS -ne 0 ]]; then
+  exit "$REPO_STATUS"
+fi
